tipRules.tip: Yamamoto pro Hite: 'I really like the Kut Tail Worm on a Neko instead of a regular Senko — the skinnier tail that tapers down has much more action when shaking along the bottom.' Best right after spawn through summer
Yamamoto pro Hite: 'I'll still use the Senko on Neko when it is colder or when I need a subtle action from my bait'
Yamamoto pro Hite: 'I like the regular Zako on my Jack Hammer ChatterBaits — the ChatterBait has so much vibration and gives the Zako a nice natural action'

Yamamoto pro Hite: 'I'll use the Kickin' Zako (paddle tail) with heavier 3/4 and 1-1/4 oz ChatterBaits on deep ledges where light penetration isn't good — want more vibration so bass can track it down'

Yamamoto pro Hite: 'I use the Kickin' Zako almost exclusively on swim jigs — the traditional swimbait tail gets a good kicking action and helps get bigger bites. The segmented bait folds up like an accordion for better hookup'

Yamamoto pro Hite: 'Rig the Fat Ika backward with an EWG hook weightless — it backslides away from you under docks, getting to places most baits can't. Also works around grass — pump your rod and it slides back away from you'
Yamamoto pro Hite: 'In Florida or any big bass lake, I like the 6-inch Senko — casts a long way and you can feel it in the grass much better'

Yamamoto pro Hite: 'I tear off all the tentacles on the side of the Flappin' Hog when punching mats — the larger 4.5-inch size slides through the mat better going in and coming out, making you much more efficient'
Yamamoto pro Hite: 'The Shad Shape worm is perfect for nose-hooking on a drop-shot. Up north it's a smallmouth catching machine. I use it anytime I'm vertically fishing a drop-shot'
Yamamoto pro Hite: 'I like the 3-inch Fat Senko for my Ned Rigs'

knowledge.insight: Yamamoto pro Hite: Use Kut Tail Worm on Neko rig post-spawn through summer for more aggressive action in warmer water. Switch to standard Senko on Neko when cold or when subtle action is needed
Yamamoto pro Hite: Standard Zako for ChatterBaits (subtle natural action), Kickin' Zako (paddle tail) for deep heavy ChatterBaits on ledges with poor light penetration and for swim jigs exclusively

Yamamoto pro Hite: The Fat Ika rigged backward weightless on EWG hook backslides like the old 'Flying Lure' — heavy salt-loaded plastic sinks on its own, and backward rigging makes it slide under docks and away from you
